Okay, last part of onboarding is your employee handbook. Okay, and so I wanted to let you all know that you do need one of these to protect yourself legally. This, depending on the state you're in and the business that you are running, Jim's obviously, but depending on your state, there's different things that you need to include here. So I'm providing you an example to reference, but please, like I am not advising you on any legal matters and I would never try to. I do suggest that you reach out to either, whoever helps you with HR, your payroll, or local counsel general. It doesn't have to be anything crazy to help you provide your team with a good employee handbook that doesn't only communicate things well to them, but also protects you legally.
